But after we close the sale, the fifth piece of the puzzle, actually the sixth piece of the puzzle is the morning after. What does that mean? Well, I think it's the, you bought what conversation, you know? Right. And it's... That's it. You've got that in there actually. Right, right. So it's the, you did what, what happened, what did you buy? And it allows the person to sell his support network or sell people in his peer group. It allows him to justify the sale. Plus it allows us, it goes back to the money. You know, second money's easier to get than first money. And so it allows us to go up the pedestal and get more and more of their dollars. So we're not going to be discussing that during this, during the DVD portion of this. What we're going to do is we're going to do a bonus call later on, which we'll tell folks about. We're going to be doing a bonus call where we're discussing actual strategies that, how to eliminate buyers or morse is really what that is. Dan, anything to add to that? Well, so it's actually three things. So it is the elimination of buyers remorse. So in some environments that's about actual refund. Oh, I came to an online launch and I made a purchase and I wake up tomorrow morning and I work the hell. So, or I went to a physical room and even if I didn't spend money, I booked, I went to a dog and pony show and I booked an appointment for three days from now for, to come in for a diagnostic process with a financial advisor and I wake up the next morning and I don't want to do that, right? It seemed like a good idea in the room. But now that I think about, now I got this questionnaire, I got to gather up all these records, I got to go in there and strip naked, I got out, I want to do that. So it is about buyers remorse. It is to his point about second money, we'll use his term, second money, which done right is easier to get than the first money, right? So it is about second money. And the third thing it's about is future customer value and or referral value because that really gets put in place as a possibility or not a possibility the morning after. So it is really about all three of those. Fantastic. And again, I think the really important point here, which is we're going to get to some questions in just a second, but a really important point is there are sales all along the way. So I don't, I'm not sure that the financial advisor actually thinks of, well, I'm actually selling the appointment and I've got to do everything I can to keep that sale before I go to the next sale and the sales process. Everything is selling. When the person, the note I took here is a front desk person who answers the call, they're greets the person when they come in, they're selling, aren't they? I mean, everything has got to be scripted. All of this has to be scripted. In the script book here that you have, Nick, you've got that for when somebody bought, for example, a launch product for that, when somebody becomes a new member. And those are all different scripts and they all have a specific outcome. You know, at the end of the day, sometimes sales people have a tendency to rush through the process. And this really, you know, if you have a system, it allows them to kind of take a conscious step by step through the process. But really the sixth step is the most, one of the most important things too, because no one wants refunds. No one really wants to get the ball over the goal line than to see the flag come and have it pulled back. And you know, then they're head under the hood and they're looking at everything. So I think, you know, it is the key and the pros really know this and really know how to romance the morning after, so to speak, and make it just as much fun as the evening before.
